The city council last night prac.
tically doctored to death the in.
itiative and referendum amend
ment to the city charter by requir'
ing petitions to be signed by 80
per cent of the voters. This prac-
tically kills the amendment, as 1t
would entail tremendous expense
and be almost impossible to secure
the required number of signatures
to petitions for referendum legisla.
ton.


The action OF the democratic
councilmen last night is regarded
as evidence of party dishonesty II.
disregard for platforms and
pledges.


Oregon cities require only 10 per
cent to initiate legislation and eight
per cent to demand a referendum.
No one has heard Of the law being
abused, as Councilman Baldwin
said would be the case II Is per
cent was all that was required.


The action of the council las,
night will find little favor with the
advocates of the proposed law.
